Notice Of Hearing On Deft'S 1) Petition To Compel Arbitration And 2) Motion To Stay Proceedings; Compendium Of Non-California Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-17-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LEHMAN BROTHERS, INC.'S DEFENDANTS' 1) PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND 2) MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.
APPEARANCES: CHRISTOPHER P. MADER, ESQUIRE (BALDWIN LAW GROUP, 650.326.8195) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. BENJAMIN W. WHITE, ESQUIRE (KEESAL YOUNG & LOGAN, 415.398.6000) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. The motion is granted. Although P's claim centers on D's recommendation to become a limited partner of Tenet, P's investment in Tenet is governed by the agreements that P signed. In those agreements, D is described as the prime broker, the general partner is authorized to act on behalf of the partnership and each limited partner appoints the general partner to sign agreements on behalf of the limited partners. One of those agreements is to arbitrate a broad range of disputes with the broker D. P's claim centers on the loss of money due to the investment in Tenet as a consequence of investments that Tenet made after P became a limited partner.
The contention that the arbitration agreement is not enforceable because D terminated the relationship with Tenet is not supported by admissible evidence. Assuming it is, the record is undisputed that at the time P invested not only was there a Tenet account at D, but P's funds were placed in that account and were governed by the documents P executed when P became a limited partner. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CSR 7433

Notice Of Hearing On Deft'S 1) Petition To Compel Arbitration And 2) Motion To Stay Proceedings; Compendium Of Non-California Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-16-2007 Continued To Dec-17-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LEHMAN BROTHERS INC.'S 1) Petition To Compel Arbitration And 2) Motion To Stay Proceedings.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - CONTINuED TO DEC. 17, 2007. OPPOSING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES WITH A COVER LETTER RFLECTING NEW DATE. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
